title: What Is AI?
summary: Students learn that AI is a kind of computer helper made by people that can use examples or patterns to help with a task.
learning_objectives: Students identify AI as a computer helper made by people and distinguish AI helpers from people and ordinary tools.
essential_question: What makes something an AI helper?
i_can_statement: I can say that AI is a computer helper made by people and explain one example.
student_challenge: Sort picture examples into people helpers; ordinary tools; and AI helpers.
student_output: Class sorting conversation and one simple oral explanation from students.
success_criteria_json: {"criteria":["Says AI is a computer helper made by people","Sorts at least one example into a sensible group","Uses because language to explain a choice"]}
alignment_json: {"core_ai_concepts":["AI is made by people","AI can use examples or patterns to help with a task","AI is not a person"],"skeleton_threads":[{"thread":"AI as a helper","level":"early_primary"}],"competency_focus":["recognize_ai_examples","compare_people_tools_ai","explain_reasoning"],"external_framework_tags":["AI literacy foundations","digital citizenship foundations"],"builds_on":[],"leads_to":["CM-AIL-G1-U1-L2"]}
localization_json: {"mena_context":"Use familiar school and home examples such as a teacher; pencil; calculator; voice helper; map app; and translation app.","language_integration":"Let students first explain in the classroom language they are most comfortable using; then model the English sentence frame.","vocabulary":[{"term":"AI","student_friendly":"a computer helper made by people"},{"term":"helper","student_friendly":"something or someone that helps us do a task"},{"term":"tool","student_friendly":"something people use to do work"}],"read_aloud_note":"Read each picture card aloud and check students understand the example before sorting."}
logistics_json: {"room_setup":"Whole class facing the board with space for three sorting groups.","grouping":"Whole class with brief partner talk.","tool_setup":"No student devices are needed. Open the smartboard sorting activity if a board is available.","data_privacy_setup":"Do not ask students to share personal accounts; names; photos; locations; or family details.","materials_manifest":["smartboard sorting activity","printable picture cards or sorting mat","teacher observation checklist"]}
materials_needed: Smartboard or printed picture cards; three sorting labels; teacher observation checklist.
teacher_prep_notes: Prepare three labels before class: People Helpers; Ordinary Tools; AI Helpers. Keep the definition simple: AI is a computer helper made by people.

title: Patterns and Instructions
summary: Students complete simple visual patterns and connect patterns to clear instructions.
learning_objectives: Students complete simple patterns and explain how instructions can change an output.
essential_question: How do patterns help us make predictions?
i_can_statement: I can find a pattern and explain what should come next.
student_challenge: Predict what comes next in a pattern and explain the rule.
student_output: Class pattern explanation and pair worksheet.
success_criteria_json: {"criteria":["Completes a simple visual pattern","Explains the pattern rule aloud","Connects clear instructions to expected output"]}
alignment_json: {"core_ai_concepts":["Patterns help systems make predictions","Instructions affect outputs"],"skeleton_threads":[{"thread":"Patterns and prediction","level":"early_primary"}],"competency_focus":["pattern_recognition","explain_rules"],"external_framework_tags":["AI literacy foundations"],"builds_on":["CM-AIL-G1-U1-L1"],"leads_to":["CM-AIL-G1-U1-L3"]}
localization_json: {"mena_context":"Use classroom colors, shapes, and familiar daily routines as pattern examples.","language_integration":"Let students describe pattern rules orally before writing or drawing.","vocabulary":[{"term":"pattern","arabic":"pattern","kurdish":"pattern"},{"term":"instruction","arabic":"instruction","kurdish":"instruction"}],"read_aloud_note":"Model one sentence frame: The pattern is... so next I think..."}
logistics_json: {"room_setup":"Students can sit in pairs with a clear view of the board.","grouping":"Whole-class modeling followed by pairs.","tool_setup":"Prepare two visible pattern examples.","data_privacy_setup":"No accounts or student data are required.","materials_manifest":["pattern cards","counters or drawings","pair worksheet"]}
materials_needed: Pattern cards; counters or drawings; printable pair worksheet.
teacher_prep_notes: Prepare one visible class pattern and one incomplete pattern for pair talk.
